_comment: CMS-SNES v1 — Canonical Machine State schema for plain LoROM/HiROM SNES (Spike T2.0). Architectural state ONLY: every field here is hardware-real. Emulator-internal fields live in the per-pair mapping files (mapping_mesen2_bsnes.json) with synthesis rules, never in the CMS. Classification key: 'architectural' = steady hardware state, portable by definition; 'ambiguous_with_rule' = hardware-real but transient/sub-instruction — portable only under the stated rule. Semantics citations are section names in fullsnes (nocash SNES hardware spec; SuperForge docs/reference/fullsnes.txt). Format truth is NEVER this file: it is the pinned vendored sources cited in the mapping file.

cart_scope.refuse_by_default: Any coprocessor presence (SA-1, SuperFX/GSU, DSP-n, CX4, OBC-1, S-DD1, SPC7110, ST-01x, SGB/ICD, MSU1, BS-X, exp-port devices) refuses transmutation loudly and leaves the native state untouched. Enumerated allowlist grows only by deliberate, validated addition.

capture_contract.point: Instruction-boundary capture on the 65C816, with the SPC700 at a cycle-consistent catch-up point. Quiescent profile (StateProbe v0) additionally requires: no DMA/HDMA transfer in flight, ALU mul/div idle, auto-joypad read not active, no half-written write-twice latch, SPC700 at an instruction boundary, no in-flight CPU->APU port write.
capture_contract.rationale: Both pilot emulators capture at CPU instruction boundaries (Mesen2: frame-edge or debugger-park; bsnes: runToSave thread-sync). Sub-instruction CPU state is therefore never portable and CMS does not model it. Sub-instruction APU/DSP pipeline state IS captured by both and IS modeled (dsp_internal).

fields.semantics: Video RAM, 64 KiB as 32K words (word-addressed by PPU)
Palette RAM, 256 BGR555 entries; bit 15 reads back as PPU2 open-bus on hardware — canonicalize masked to 15 bits
Object Attribute Memory: 512-byte low table (128 sprites x 4 bytes) + 32-byte high table (x-MSB + size bits)
Audio RAM (SPC700 address space). The $FFC0-$FFFF window content is the RAM UNDER the IPL ROM; visibility is governed by apu_io.ipl_rom_enable
S-DSP register file $00-$7F as last written (VxVOL/VxPITCH/VxSRCN/VxADSR/VxGAIN, ENVX/OUTX, MVOL/EVOL, KON/KOF, FLG, ENDX, EFB, PMON, NON, EON, DIR, ESA, EDL, FIR coefficients)

dsp_internal._comment: Hardware-real DSP engine state below register granularity. Both pilot emulators model the same silicon pipeline (blargg-lineage), so these map nearly 1:1; they are 'internal' to the DSP chip, not to any emulator. All are ambiguous_with_rule ONLY in the sense that they advance every DSP tick; at any capture point they have exact values both sides represent.

fields.semantics: Per-voice BRR decode ring buffer (12 decoded samples)
Per-voice current BRR block address in ARAM
Per-voice byte offset within current BRR block (1..8)
Per-voice internal envelope accumulator (persists through GAIN direct writes)
Per-voice last ENVX value latched for register readback
8-tap stereo FIR echo filter history ring
Current byte offset within the echo region (advances by 4 per sample)
Latched echo region length (EDL captured at region wrap)
Global rate counter driving envelope/noise timing (period 30720)
1-bit toggle: KON/KOF are sampled every other DSP sample
KON bits written since last poll

fields.rule: Intra-sample pipeline step (0..31 DSP clocks per sample pair). Both sides represent it; copy directly. Quiescent captures SHOULD land at a stable phase but any value is representable — divergence here is bounded to one output sample.
fields.semantics: Position within the 32-clock DSP sample cycle

Per-clock pipeline latches (t_pmon, t_non, t_eon, t_dir, t_koff, t_brr_next_addr, t_adsr0, t_brr_header, t_brr_byte, t_srcn, t_esa, t_echo_enabled, t_dir_addr, t_pitch, t_output, t_looped, t_echo_ptr, main_out[2], echo_out[2], echo_in[2]). Same silicon model both sides; copy field-for-field per mapping table.
APU->CPU mailbox: SPC-side writes to $F4-$F7, visible at $2140-43
CPU->APU mailbox: CPU-side writes to $2140-43, visible at $F4-$F7
$F1 CONTROL: timer enables, IPL ROM enable (port-clear strobes are write-transient, not state)
$F0 TEST: internal/external wait states, RAM enable/writable, timer disable. Nonstandard values are hardware-legal but games never use them; canonical value $0A
Timer prescaler + divider stage counters (stage0/1/2). Hardware-real counters below register granularity; both sides model them — copy per mapping

fields.rule: Read-to-clear output counters $FD-$FF. Copy value directly; the READ side-effect is a capture-protocol concern (a probe that read them just before capture sees them cleared), not a mapping concern.

fields.semantics: Battery RAM view embedded in the state. Size from cart header (StateProbe: 8 KiB). T2 open question (design doc): refresh-with-current-SRAM at rebuild time — StateProbe's seeded SRAM audit answers it in P2/P3
$4200: NMI enable, V/H IRQ enable, auto-joypad enable
$4201 programmable I/O port out (bit7 latches counters on 1->0)
$4207/08 H-IRQ dot position (bsnes stores premultiplied x4; see mapping transform)

fields.rule: GP-DMA enable bits written but transfer not yet started (start delay). Quiescent captures require 0; otherwise map with the dma domain's in-flight rule.
$4210 bit7 NMI-occurred flag (cleared on read). Copy directly; interacts with nmi edge pipeline — see mapping interrupt truth table.
$4211 bit7 IRQ-occurred flag (cleared on read). Copy directly; see interrupt truth table.

Internal NMI edge-detector pipeline (line level, transition pending, hold). Microarchitecture differs per emulator; mapped via documented truth table in the mapping file. Quiescent beacon = no interrupt transition in flight.
Same as nmi_line_state for H/V IRQ.

In-flight mul (8 cyc) / div (16 cyc) countdown + shift accumulator. Both emulators model the same iterative silicon: map counters+shift directly. Quiescent captures require idle (counters 0). StateProbe v3 exercises non-idle deliberately.

Auto-joypad read runs the first ~4224 master cycles of vblank; a vblank-start capture can be mid-read. Both sides serialize progress (counter/clock + port shift latches). Map per table; quiescent beacon must sit OUTSIDE the auto-read window (StateProbe parks beacon accordingly).
fields.semantics: Auto-joypad read progress + controller latch lines

irq_nmi_cadence._comment: Covered by cpu_io.nmi_line_state / irq_line_state / rdnmi_flag / timeup_flag plus timing domain. StateProbe domain 9 audits the cadence behaviorally.

fields.semantics: Master-clock position within scanline (0..1363; both pilot emulators count master clocks intra-line)
Odd/even frame flip-flop (long/short dot on NTSC, interlace field pairing)

mailbox_protocol._comment: Behavioral domain (StateProbe sequence counters ride in WRAM/ARAM). The state carrier is apu.port_to_cpu/port_from_cpu; one hazard field:

fields.rule: Mesen2 models a 1-cycle-delayed CPU->APU port write (pending flag + staged values); bsnes applies writes at sync granularity. Quiescent rule: pending MUST be false at capture (StateProbe v0 guarantees; decode refuses otherwise or flushes staged values into port_from_cpu after verifying the staged-write semantics in P2).

fields.semantics: $43x0 DMAP: direction, indirect, addressing mode, fixed, decrement, transfer pattern
$43x5/6 byte count (GP-DMA, counts down) / indirect address (HDMA)
$43x8/9 current HDMA table address (advances per line)

fields.rule: A GP-DMA mid-transfer or HDMA mid-line-setup at capture. Quiescent rule: nothing in flight (StateProbe v0 guarantees; frame-edge capture makes GP-DMA-in-flight impossible unless the game started one spanning vblank entry). Per-frame HDMA setup/run event positions are emulator bookkeeping synthesized from timing domain on encode; the per-channel table/line state above is architectural and carries.

fields.semantics: $2101 OBJ size select, name base, name select
$2102/03 OAM address reload value + priority-rotation enable
Live internal OAM word/byte address (advances on writes; reset at vblank per reload)
Low-byte latch for paired OAM low-table writes (write-twice hazard target)
$2105 BG mode + BG3 priority + tile size bits

fields.rule: Mosaic row phase (scanline counter within mosaic block). Hardware-real; both sides model (Mesen2 _mosaicScanlineCounter, bsnes mosaic.vcounter). Copy with the timing domain; only meaningful mid-frame.

fields.semantics: Shared write-twice scroll latch (previous byte written) — StateProbe v1 half-write hazard target
VRAM read prefetch buffer (dummy-read quirk state) — StateProbe v1 hazard target
$211B-1E M7A-M7D (also mul input: M7A x M7B.high -> $2134-36)
CGRAM low-byte write latch — StateProbe v1 hazard target
$2131 color math enable per layer + subtract + halve
PPU1 open-bus data register ($2134-36 et al. readback)

epoch_continuity._comment: Pure behavioral domain: StateProbe's own WRAM epoch counter must continue monotonically after transfer. No state fields beyond wram.

excluded_domains._comment: Enumerated exclusions with reason. Presence of any of these in a source state (or cart) => REFUSE.
excluded_domains.coprocessor_state: Chip firewall (design doc): SA-1, GSU/SuperFX, DSP-n, CX4, OBC-1, S-DD1, SPC7110, ST-01x, SGB/ICD, MSU1, BS-X, Sufami Turbo.
excluded_domains.cpu_sub_instruction: 65C816 mid-instruction state (cycle-within-opcode, internal temporaries, pending bus transaction). Unportable by capture contract; capture protocol guarantees instruction boundary.
excluded_domains.spc_sub_instruction: SPC700 mid-instruction micro-step (Mesen2 models per-cycle opStep/opSubStep + operand temporaries; bsnes is instruction-atomic). Quiescent rule: Mesen2 opStep MUST equal instruction-start at capture, else refuse (replaying a partial instruction can double-consume read-to-clear APU registers). StateProbe beacon design makes this constructible; frame-edge captures empirically land here (P2 will measure how often).
excluded_domains.emulator_framebuffers: Rendered output is derived state; regenerated within one frame.
excluded_domains.emulator_scheduling: Cycle accounting, event queues, thread/cothread state, clock ratios: synthesized by the target's own power-on + load path (donor template).
